topic_name: Wives Daughters Sons of Holy Prophet
subject: islamic-studies
description: Wives Daughters Sons of Holy Prophet MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: How many wives did the Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) have?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) had 12 wives in total. All of his wives are honoured with the title Umm-ul-Momineen (Mother of the Believers), as stated in Surah Al-Ahzab (33:6).

mcqs.q: What title is given to all wives of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: All wives of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) are given the title Umm-ul-Momineen, meaning 'Mother of the Believers.' This title is bestowed in the Quran (Surah Al-Ahzab 33:6) and signifies their special status in the Muslim community.

mcqs.q: Who was the first wife of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah bint Khuwaylid (RA) was the first wife of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She married him in 595 AD at age 40. She was the first person to accept Islam and supported the Prophet through the earliest and most difficult years of his mission.

mcqs.q: What was the title of Hazrat Khadijah (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) was given the title At-Tahira (The Pious One). She belonged to the tribe of Banu Asad. Sadiqqa is the title of Ayesha (RA); Umm-ul-Masaakeen is Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA); Khatoon-e-Jannat is Fatima (RA).

mcqs.q: To which tribe did Hazrat Khadijah (RA) belong?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) belonged to the tribe of Banu Asad. She was a wealthy and respected merchant in Makkah before her marriage to the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: How many years older was Hazrat Khadijah (RA) than the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) was 15 years older than the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She was 40 years old at the time of marriage in 595 AD, while the Prophet was 25 years old.

mcqs.q: How long did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Hazrat Khadijah (RA) live together?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Hazrat Khadijah (RA) lived together for 25 years — from their marriage in 595 AD until her death in 619 AD. The Prophet did not marry another woman during her lifetime.

mcqs.q: Who was the first person to accept Islam?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) was the first person to accept Islam — she immediately believed when the Prophet returned from the Cave of Hira with the first revelation. Among men, Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) is considered first; among youth, Hazrat Ali (RA).

mcqs.q: On which date did Hazrat Khadijah (RA) pass away?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) passed away on 10th Ramadan 619 AD. This event, along with the death of the Prophet's uncle Abu Talib in the same year, led to this year being called Aam-ul-Huzn (Year of Grief) by Muslims.

mcqs.q: Where is Hazrat Khadijah (RA) buried?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) is buried at Jannat-e-Moalla in Makkah. Jannat-ul-Baqi is the main cemetery in Madinah where many companions and family members of the Prophet are buried, including Hazrat Saudah (RA).

mcqs.q: Who carried the message of nikah (marriage proposal) from Hazrat Khadijah (RA) to the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Nafeesa, a friend of Hazrat Khadijah (RA), carried the marriage message from Khadijah to the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She was also known as Nafeesah bint Munyah.

mcqs.q: What was given to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) as dower (mahr) by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave Hazrat Khadijah (RA) a dower of 20 camels and 12 ounces of silver. This is a recorded fact frequently tested in competitive examinations on Islamic history.

mcqs.q: Who was the first person to offer prayer (Salat) among the Ummah of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Khadijah (RA) was the first person to offer Salat (prayer) among the Ummah of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She accepted Islam immediately and began praying alongside the Prophet from the very beginning.

mcqs.q: Did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) offer the funeral prayer of Hazrat Khadijah (RA)?
options: No, because the rule of funeral prayer was not yet revealed
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) did not offer the funeral prayer of Hazrat Khadijah (RA) — this was by Allah's will. The funeral prayer (Salat-ul-Janazah) rules were formalised after the Hijra, but this is recorded as a specific fact about Khadijah's funeral.

mcqs.q: At what age did Hazrat Saudah bint Zam'ah (RA), the second wife, marry the Holy Prophet?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Saudah bint Zam'ah (RA) married the Holy Prophet (PBUH) at age 50, in the 10th year of Prophethood. She was the first woman the Prophet married after the death of Hazrat Khadijah (RA).

mcqs.q: Where is Hazrat Saudah bint Zam'ah (RA) buried?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Saudah bint Zam'ah (RA) is buried at Jannat-ul-Baqi in Madinah, the main cemetery beside Masjid-e-Nabawi. She died during the last days of the caliphate of Hazrat Umer (RA).

mcqs.q: What was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ayesha (RA) was given the title Sadiqqa (the truthful woman). She is also called Al-Tayyabeen. At-Tahira is Khadijah's title; Umm-ul-Masaakeen is Zainab bint Khuzaima's title; Khatoon-e-Jannat is Fatima's title.

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Ayesha (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ayesha (RA) narrated 2210 ahadith — the most among all wives of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She is the third largest narrator of hadith overall. The largest narrator among all companions is Hazrat Abu Hurairah (RA) with 5374.

mcqs.q: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) was buried in whose room (hujra)?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) was buried in the hujra (room) of Hazrat Ayesha (RA) in Madinah. His room is now incorporated within Masjid-e-Nabawi. Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) and Hazrat Umer (RA) are also buried nearby.

mcqs.q: Who was the first woman to lead an Islamic army?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ayesha (RA) was the first woman to lead an Islamic army, during the Battle of Jamal (Jang-e-Jamal) in 36 AH. She rode a camel into battle, which is why it is called the Battle of the Camel.

mcqs.q: The 'Incident of Ifk' refers to a slander against which wife of the Prophet?
mcqs.explanation: The Incident of Ifk (slander) refers to a false accusation made by Abdullah bin Ubay (a hypocrite) against Hazrat Ayesha (RA). Allah revealed verses in Surah An-Nur (24:11–20) declaring her innocence.

mcqs.q: Which wife of the Holy Prophet is famous as the guardian of the first written Quran?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Hafsa bint Umer (RA), the fourth wife of the Holy Prophet, is famous as the guardian of the first written Quran. The compiled manuscript was kept in her custody during the caliphate of Abu Bakr (RA) and Umer (RA).

mcqs.q: Who was the father of Hazrat Hafsa (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Hafsa bint Umer (RA) was the daughter of Hazrat Umer ibn Khattab (RA), the second Caliph of Islam. Her mother was Zainab bint Mazun (RA). She was widowed after her first husband Khunais ibn-e-Huzaifa was martyred.

mcqs.q: Who was the first husband of Hazrat Hafsa (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: The first husband of Hazrat Hafsa (RA) was Khunais ibn-e-Huzaifa. He was martyred in the Battle of Badr or Uhud. After his death, the Holy Prophet (PBUH) married Hafsa (RA).

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Hafsa (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Hafsa (RA) narrated 60 ahadith. She died in 45 AH in the month of Sha'aban. She is one of the most important personalities in the preservation of the Quran.

mcqs.q: Hazrat Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA) was known as:
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA) was known as Umm-ul-Masaakeen, meaning 'Mother of the Poor,' because of her extraordinary generosity toward the destitute. She belonged to the Hilal tribe and died only 8 months after marriage.

mcqs.q: How long did Hazrat Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA) live after her marriage to the Holy Prophet?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA) passed away only 8 months after her marriage to the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She is one of only two wives of the Prophet who died during his lifetime, the other being Hazrat Khadijah (RA).

mcqs.q: What was the real name of Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: The real name of Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA) was Hind bint Abu Umayyah. She was also known as Hind al-Makhzumiyah. Her kunyah (nickname) Umm-e-Salamah became her most recognised name.

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA) narrated 378 ahadith — the second highest among all wives of the Holy Prophet after Hazrat Ayesha (RA). She died at the age of 84 during the reign of Yazid bin Muawiyyah, after the Karbala incident.

mcqs.q: Who was the first husband of Hazrat Zainab bint Jahash (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: The first husband of Hazrat Zainab bint Jahash (RA) was Zaid bin Harith (RA), who was the adopted son of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). After their divorce, Allah commanded the Prophet to marry Zainab — this is referenced in Surah Al-Ahzab (33:37).

mcqs.q: What was the actual name of Hazrat Juwayriya bint al-Harith (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: The actual name of Hazrat Juwayriya (RA) was Barrah. The Holy Prophet (PBUH) changed her name to Juwayriya. She belonged to the Banu Mustaliq tribe and was initially a war captive. She narrated 7 ahadith.

mcqs.q: To which tribe did Hazrat Juwayriya bint al-Harith (RA) belong?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Juwayriya (RA) belonged to the Banu Mustaliq tribe. Her father al-Harith ibn Abi Dirar was the chief of the tribe. Her marriage to the Prophet led to the release of many Banu Mustaliq captives by the Companions.

mcqs.q: Hazrat Ramlah bint Abu Sufyan (RA) is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ramlah bint Abu Sufyan (RA) is also known as Umm-e-Habiba — her kunyah derived from her daughter Habiba. She was the daughter of Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, who was a staunch opponent of Islam before his later conversion.

mcqs.q: Which wife of the Holy Prophet was the daughter of Abu Sufyan?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ramlah bint Abu Sufyan (RA), also known as Umm-e-Habiba, was the daughter of Abu Sufyan ibn Harb (the Quraysh leader). She had accepted Islam early and emigrated to Abyssinia. She narrated 65 ahadith.

mcqs.q: Hazrat Safiyah (RA) was the daughter of the chief of which Jewish tribe?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Safiyah (RA) was the daughter of Huyayy ibn Akhtab, who was the chief of the Jewish tribe Banu Nadir based in Madinah. She accepted Islam after the Battle of Khaybar and married the Prophet.

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Maymunah bint al-Haarith (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Maymunah bint al-Haarith (RA) narrated 76 ahadith. She was the 11th wife of the Holy Prophet (PBUH), the daughter of Al-Harith ibn Hazn and Hind bint Awf.

mcqs.q: How many daughters did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) have?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) had 4 daughters: Hazrat Zainab (RA) — eldest, Hazrat Ruqayya (RA) — second, Hazrat Umme-Kulsoom (RA) — third, and Hazrat Fatima (RA) — youngest. All four were born of Hazrat Khadijah (RA).

mcqs.q: Who was the eldest daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab (RA) was the eldest daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She married her maternal cousin Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi' and had two children: Ali bin Zainab (son) and Umamah bint Zainab (daughter).

mcqs.q: Who did Hazrat Zainab (RA), the eldest daughter, marry?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab (RA), the eldest daughter of the Holy Prophet, married Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi', who was her maternal cousin. He later accepted Islam.

mcqs.q: Which daughter of the Holy Prophet married Hazrat Uthman bin Affan (RA) first?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ruqayya (RA), the second daughter of the Holy Prophet, was the first to marry Hazrat Uthman bin Affan (RA). After Ruqayya's death, Hazrat Umme-Kulsoom (RA) also married Uthman — earning him the title Dhul-Noorayn.

mcqs.q: Why is Hazrat Uthman bin Affan (RA) called Dhul-Noorayn?
options: He had two swords in battle
He married two daughters of the Holy Prophet
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Uthman (RA) is called Dhul-Noorayn (Possessor of Two Lights) because he married two daughters of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) — first Hazrat Ruqayya (RA), and after her death, Hazrat Umme-Kulsoom (RA). This honour is unique to him.

mcqs.q: What is the title of Hazrat Fatima (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Fatima (RA), the youngest daughter of the Holy Prophet, is known as Khatoon-e-Jannat (Lady of Paradise). She is also called Al-Zahra (the radiant) and Fatimah al-Batool.

mcqs.q: When was Hazrat Fatima (RA) born?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Fatima (RA) was born in 605 AD in Makkah, five years before the beginning of the Prophet's mission (610 AD). She is the youngest of the Holy Prophet's four daughters.

mcqs.q: Who did Hazrat Fatima (RA) marry and in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Fatima (RA) married Hazrat Ali ibn Talib (RA) in 623 AD (2 AH), in Madinah. Their marriage is considered a model of Islamic family life. Hazrat Ali was the cousin and son-in-law of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: How many sons did Hazrat Fatima (RA) have?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Fatima (RA) had 3 sons: Hazrat Hassan (RA), Hazrat Hussain (RA), and Hazrat Moshin (RA). She also had 2 daughters: Zainab (RA) and Umme-Kulsoom (RA).

mcqs.q: How long after the Holy Prophet (PBUH) did Hazrat Fatima (RA) pass away?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Fatima (RA) passed away 6 months after the Holy Prophet (PBUH). The Prophet died in 11 AH (632 AD), and Fatima (RA) passed away in the same year. She is buried at Jannat-ul-Baqi in Madinah.

mcqs.q: How many sons did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) have?
mcqs.explanation: The Holy Prophet (PBUH) had 3 sons: Hazrat Qasim (RA) — eldest, Hazrat Abdullah (RA) — second, and Hazrat Ibrahim (RA) — youngest. All three sons passed away in infancy.

mcqs.q: Who was the eldest son of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Qasim (RA) was the eldest son of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). He was born of Hazrat Khadijah (RA) and is buried at Janat-al-Mualla in Makkah. The Prophet was known as Abu Qasim (father of Qasim).

mcqs.q: Where is Hazrat Qasim (RA), the eldest son, buried?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Qasim (RA) is buried at Janat-al-Mualla in Makkah, the same cemetery where Hazrat Khadijah (RA) is buried. Hazrat Abdullah (RA), the second son, is also buried there alongside him.

mcqs.q: Who was the youngest son of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ibrahim (RA) was the youngest son of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). He was born of Mariyah al-Qibtiyyah (RA), a Coptic Egyptian. He passed away in infancy at about 18 months of age.

mcqs.q: Which wife of the Holy Prophet narrated the most ahadith?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ayesha (RA) narrated 2210 ahadith — the most among all wives of the Holy Prophet. The second highest is Umm-e-Salamah with 378, followed by Maymunah (76), Ramlah/Umm-e-Habiba (65), and Hafsa (60).

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Zainab bint Jahash (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab bint Jahash (RA) narrated 11 ahadith. She died during the caliphate of Hazrat Umer (RA). She was the daughter of Jahash ibn Riyab and Umayma bint Abdul Muttalib (an aunt of the Prophet).

mcqs.q: How many ahadith did Hazrat Juwayriya (RA) narrate?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Juwayriya bint al-Harith (RA) narrated 7 ahadith — the fewest among the wives for whom narration counts are recorded. Her actual name was Barrah; the Prophet renamed her Juwayriya.

mcqs.q: Which wife of the Holy Prophet died 8 months after their marriage?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Zainab bint Khuzaima (RA) passed away only 8 months after her marriage to the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She is one of two wives who died during the Prophet's lifetime, along with Hazrat Khadijah (RA).

mcqs.q: At what age did Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA) pass away?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Umm-e-Salamah (RA) passed away at the age of 84 during the era of Yazid bin Muawiyyah, after the tragedy of Karbala. She is noted for being one of the longest-lived among the wives of the Prophet.

mcqs.q: What tribe did Hazrat Ayesha (RA) belong to?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ayesha (RA) belonged to the Banu Tamim tribe. She was born in 614 CE in Makkah. Her father was Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) and her mother was Umm-e-Ruman Zainab (RA).

mcqs.q: Who first informed Hazrat Ayesha (RA) about the slander (Incident of Ifk)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ummay Mustah bint Asasah (RA) was the first person to inform Hazrat Ayesha (RA) about the slander that was being spread (Incident of Ifk). Allah then revealed verses in Surah An-Nur declaring Ayesha's complete innocence.

mcqs.q: Which daughter of the Holy Prophet is known as Khatoon-e-Jannat?
mcqs.explanation: Khatoon-e-Jannat (Lady of Paradise) is the title of Hazrat Fatima (RA), the youngest and most famous daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). She married Hazrat Ali ibn Talib (RA) and is the mother of Hassan (RA) and Hussain (RA).

mcqs.q: Which two daughters of the Holy Prophet married Hazrat Uthman (RA)?
mcqs.explanation: Hazrat Ruqayya (RA) was the first to marry Hazrat Uthman (RA). After her death, Hazrat Umme-Kulsoom (RA) married him. Because of this unique honour — marrying two daughters of a Prophet — Hazrat Uthman earned the title Dhul-Noorayn.

mcqs.q: Where are Hazrat Qasim (RA) and Hazrat Abdullah (RA), sons of the Holy Prophet, buried?
mcqs.explanation: Both Hazrat Qasim (RA) and Hazrat Abdullah (RA), the first and second sons of the Holy Prophet (PBUH), are buried at Janat-al-Mualla in Makkah — the same cemetery where Hazrat Khadijah (RA) is buried.
